element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns to dodge and shoot simultaneously across foreground-background depth
Player learns to catch and throw back enemy explosives
Increased firing speed and damage output
Shooting enemy bullets fills gauge; reward is temporary Vulcan gun with invincibility
Complex patterns combining all learned mechanics

typical_relief_triggers: Acquiring Vulcan gun via bullet-deflection gauge
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based escalation with foreground-background depth creating constant threat assessment; shooting and moving are mutually exclusive, forcing rhythm of aim-and-hold vs. dodge-and-move cycles

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to dodge mechanics and basic enemy patterns; 'Look Out!' text bubble aids player judgment of bullet depth and distance
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Weapon upgrades and multi-enemy coordination; bullet-deflection gauge mechanic adds skill-based reward system
difficulty_curve.late_game: Mini-bosses and final bosses combine all learned mechanics with complex attack patterns
notable_spikes: Mini-boss transitions (end of stage 2 each level)
Screen shaking and mirage effects post-explosion create visual disorientation

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of 6 levels contains stage 1 (standard enemies), stage 2 (enemy waves + mini-boss), stage 3 (final boss). Consistent structure with escalating difficulty within each level

Player in foreground with D-pad sidestep and jump; enemies and bullets in background. Artificial 3D depth requires constant threat assessment. Originally lateral movement was sidestepping; reticle was changed to move laterally while aiming to reduce cumbersomeness

Player cannot shoot and move simultaneously. While gun is holstered, D-pad controls movement/jumping/diving/rolling. While aiming (holding fire), D-pad moves reticle. Creates rhythm of aim-hold cycles vs. dodge-move cycles
Maximum 5 bombs at a time for screen clear. Bomb boxes spawn from defeated enemies. Players balance bomb usage with available resources to manage peak threat moments

sources_used: Wikipedia - Wild Guns (Gameplay and Development sections)
The Cutting Room Floor - Wild Guns (stage structure information)
